responses.note: Chose past perfect 'had sat' for past continuous interrupted-action slot; target B 'was sitting'. Production analog (item 06) deployed correctly — recognition-vs-production inverse pattern (cf. v1 Comparatives Δ −33 pts cluster).

responses.response: I sent the draft contract yesterday in the evening.
responses.note: Past simple form clean. 'in the evening' is grammatical but slightly non-native — native default 'yesterday evening' or 'last night'. PS form is the target; not penalised.

responses.response: When the client called, I was riding a bike.
responses.note: Past continuous deployed cleanly. Note inverse pattern with item 04 — same structural template, recognition missed but production landed.

responses.response: We selected another contractor last month.
responses.note: PS irregular form not required ('selected' is regular). Form clean either way.

responses.response: I used to ride by bike every morning to work.
responses.note: 'used to ride' deployed correctly. 'by bike' is a mild idiom miss — native 'ride my bike' / 'cycle' / 'ride to work by bike' (positional). Tense/aspect target is met; partial for idiomatic phrasing only.

responses.response: By Friday we had agreed all final terms with the regulator.
responses.note: KEY RESULT: past perfect deployed CLEANLY in production with time anchor ('by Friday'). Russian 'к пятнице' triggers past perfect correctly. This is the bridge-to-B2 item — landing it cleanly refines v1's past_perfect_malformed_attempt finding (items 19, 34 v1 with 'had been already left') — the form basics are NOT the gap; the v1 failure was specifically the compound interaction with passive/intransitive selection + 'already' word order.
